Gap Description: Full Primary Account Numbers (PANs) are stored unencrypted in the card_number column of the RDS orders table, and raw card data appears in S3 transaction logs via query string parameters.
Remediation Effort: High (schema changes, tokenization implementation, log scrubbing, data purge)

Gap Description: Internal traffic between AWS Lambda and the RDS PostgreSQL instance does not enforce TLS, meaning PANs traversing this path are transmitted in cleartext within the VPC.
Remediation Effort: Low (enable ssl=require in connection string, enforce SSL on RDS parameter group)

Gap Description: No network segmentation exists between the CDE (Lambda, RDS, S3 log path) and the rest of the AWS VPC, expanding the blast radius of any compromise.
Remediation Effort: Medium (define CDE subnet boundaries, security groups, NACLs; re-architect VPC routing)

Gap Description: The admin portal is protected by username and password only; no multi-factor authentication is enforced for access into the CDE.
Remediation Effort: Low (enable MFA via IAM/Cognito or SSO provider, enforce via policy)

Gap Description: No evidence of regular internal/external vulnerability scanning (ASV) or an annual penetration test covering the API Gateway, Lambda, or RDS.
Remediation Effort: Medium (engage ASV for quarterly scans, schedule annual pentest, remediate findings)

Gap Description: OS and dependency patching is performed roughly quarterly on an ad hoc basis, well outside PCI DSS risk-based patching timelines for critical/high vulnerabilities.
Remediation Effort: Medium (adopt automated dependency scanning, define SLA-based patch cadence, e.g. 30 days for critical)

Gap Description: CloudWatch logs are collected but never reviewed, and no alerting exists for suspicious access patterns (e.g., repeated failed logins, unusual DB queries).
Remediation Effort: Medium (configure CloudWatch alarms/metric filters, route to SIEM or on-call alerting, define review SOP)

Gap Description: No documented secure configuration baseline exists for Lambda and RDS; default parameter groups and configurations appear to be in use rather than hardened, reviewed settings.
Remediation Effort: Medium (define hardening baseline, apply custom RDS parameter group, remove default credentials/settings)

Gap Description: No least-privilege access control model or periodic access review is documented for admin portal users or IAM roles touching the CDE.
Remediation Effort: Medium (define role-based access matrix, apply least privilege to IAM policies, schedule quarterly access reviews)

Gap Description: Three third-party checkout plugins are in use in the checkout flow with no verified SAQ-A (or equivalent) compliance documentation on file.
Remediation Effort: Low (request SAQ-A/AOC from each vendor, remove or replace any that cannot provide one)
